verdict.answer_first: A's first sentence is a complete, precise definition: "Big-O describes how the *work* an algorithm does grows as the input gets bigger — not how many seconds it takes, but how the number of operations scales."
verdict.decisiveness: A provides a single, powerful heuristic to internalize: "when you see a loop nested inside another loop walking the same collection, ask 'what happens when this list is 100× bigger?'"
verdict.actionability: A is more actionable because it's structured as a concise reference. The most common performance fix is presented clearly: "The fix is almost always 'use a Set or Map to trade space for time'"
